texto: [I]t seemed to me now that a Catholic church was the right companion for all these horrors. Didn't Catholicism deal with blood and resurrected flesh on a daily basis? Wasn't it expert in superstition? I somehow doubted that the hospitable plain Protestant chapels that dotted the university could be much help
autor: they didn't look qualified to wrestle with the undead. I felt sure those big square Puritan churches on the town green would be helpless in the face of a European vampire. A little witch burning was more in their line--something limited to the neighbors.

A little witch burning was more in their line--something limited to the neighbors. que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “[I]t seemed to me now that a Catholic church was the right companion for all these horrors. Didn'…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, elizabeth-kostova, the-historian, com foco em the-historian.
